text:
The most elegant simple tool I can think of is the pencil.

It requires no power source, no connectivity, no maintenance beyond the occasional sharpening. It writes, it draws, it marks. You can erase what it leaves behind. It works at altitude, underwater with some modifications, in extreme cold where ink fails. It gets smaller as it is used, which is a kind of honest accounting of its labor. The shavings it produces smell good. When it breaks, you sharpen it and continue. There is no mode it needs to be switched into, no battery to check, no app to update.

The pencil is also a record of pressure. A light touch produces a light mark. A heavy hand leaves a dark one. It responds to the person using it in a way that most tools do not, making it both a writing implement and a mild form of self-portrait.

Its elegance comes from the fact that it does exactly one thing, does it without complaint, and asks almost nothing in return.
